stem: A software testing study surveys 25 test cases and observes 6 positive outcomes out of 25 (24.0%). The sample contains two subgroups: Group A (16 observations, rate = 18.8%) and Group B (9 observations, rate = 33.3%). The true population composition is uncertain: Group A makes up between 45% and 55% of the population. Estimate the true software bug rate and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Simple pooled estimate: 0.24. Weighted estimate (stratified): 0.2692 (range [0.2627, 0.2756]). Combined CI accounting for stratification uncertainty: [0.115, 0.4343]. Point estimate: 0.2692.

solution_steps: Stratified estimate: weighted by population composition. At w_A=0.5: 0.5×0.205 + 0.5×0.3333 = 0.2692.
common_failure_modes: Using the Wald interval (p̂ ± z√(p̂(1-p̂)/n)) which performs poorly for small samples
Not reporting the confidence interval alongside the point estimate
Not recognizing that with n=25, the normal approximation may be poor
Ignoring the population composition uncertainty and using the simple pooled rate
